title: Getting User Input 💬
description: Make your programs interactive by accepting input with gets.

slides.title: Listen to the User 👂
slides.content:
# The `gets` Method

Programs are boring if they only talk *at* you. Let's make our programs listen!

Ruby uses the `gets` command to wait for the user to type something and hit Enter.

`gets` stands for **get string**.

slides.title: Interactive Code
slides.content: Run this code. It will pause and wait for you to type your name!
slides.codeSnippet:
puts "What is your name?"
name = gets
puts "Hello " + name

slides.title: Quick Check 🧠
slides.content: Let's review the syntax for grabbing input.

quizData.question: Which method stops the program and waits for the user to type something?
quizData.explanation: `gets` waits for keyboard input. It grabs the string typed along with the 'Enter' key press.

slides.title: The Hidden New Line 🕵️‍♂️
slides.content:
# Chomp out the Enter Key

Did you notice something weird in the previous code? When you type your name and hit Enter, Ruby captures your name **AND** the Enter key press (a new line).

To strip away that invisible Enter key, we attach `.chomp` to the end of gets.

Like this:
`name = gets.chomp`

This "chomps" off the hidden new line!

slides.title: Chomping Action
slides.content: Let's see the improved version using `.chomp`.
slides.codeSnippet:
puts "What is your name?"
name = gets.chomp
puts "Hello " + name + ", you look great today!"

quizData.question: What is the purpose of adding .chomp to gets?

options.text: It turns the text into uppercase.
It chomps out any spaces in the middle of words.
It removes the hidden new-line character captured when the user hits Enter.

quizData.explanation: When you hit Enter after typing your input, a new line character (\n) is appended. `.chomp` removes it so your string formats nicely.
